California
Fair Political
Practices Cornrnission
November 29 | ]-99O
l'1r. Emery B. Do$re 11
P. O. Box !91L67
Sacramento, CA 9 5819
Dear Mr. Dovrell:
This is in response to your written request for informal
advice concerni-ng your responsibi I ities under the conflict of
i.rt.."=t 
provisi5n-s of the Political Reform Act (the. r'Actr') ' 1
Because yLu have specifically requested informal advice and you
are not iacing a specific governmental decision, we provide you
with informal advice. (Regulation 18329(c), copy enclosed' )z our
advice is Lirnited to questions arising under the- ActTr
OUESTION
To s/hat extent do you, as a member of the Major Medical
Insurance Board (the 'rlnsurance Boardrr) and as a director of Blue
c."== "t California (ttB]ue cross!t), face a disqualifying conflict
of intere-st in carrying out your duties on the Insurance Board?
CONCLUSION
Because BIue cross has been a source of income to you in the
Iast 12 months, you may not participate in any governmental-
decision in whicl Blue cross is directly involved. You may also
have a disqualifying conflict of interest if there is a rrnexusrl
Letween tn6 aecilion and the purpose for which you receive income
1 
co.r"..,ment code Sections 81ooo-91o15. A11 statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations
section 18ooo; et seq. A11 references to regulations are to
Titl,e 2, Division 6 of the California code of Regulations'
2 
Irrfoa*a1 assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formaf written advice. (section
83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3) -)
3 we offer no advice concerning Government code Section 1o9o' If
you have questions regarding application of Section l-090, you
should contact -"he Attorney General-'s office.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 o (916)322'5660
Re: Your Request
Ass i stance
our File No.
for Informal
I-90-5s6


our Fi■e No.  I-90-656
Page 2
from Blue cross, or if i'. is reasonably forseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect on Blue cross, even
if Bfue cross is only indirectly involved in the decision.
FACTS
You have been appointed to serve as a member of the Major
Medical Insurance Board, which wilI be implementing the California
Itajor Medical Insurance Program. (Insurance Code Sections 127oo,
et seq. ) The Board's duties incl,ude deterrnining what insurance
cove.ag" to provide, and deciding which insurance carriers should
receiv6 contiacts to provide the coverage. (rd., section 12710.)
According to your l-etter, the proqran is designed so the insurance
carriers-chosln by the Board will receive speci-f ic premiums, will
be reimbursed for medical costs that exceed the premiums received,
and will be paid a specific amount to cover costs of
adrninistration and claims payrnents. Your letter goes on to say'
however, that the contracts with the carriers wiII be designed to
p.""""t'any profit or l-oss to the carriers.4
During our November 9 phone conversation with your office, 
we
were informed that the Insurance Board will soon develop a
rrprototyperr contract, the final version of which wil.1 be the
c^ontracl- under which insurance carriers will ope;atd-pursuant to
the progran.
You have been a director of BIue cross for three years and
your present tern is for two more years. Blue cross, according to
|our letter, is a 501(c) (6) nonprofit entity in the heafth
irrarr.n"" business. Your letter does not expressly say so, but
ine imptication is that the Insurance Board is considering or will
cons iaLr'-awardi-ng a contract to Blue cross to effectuate the l,lajor
Medical Insurance Program.
You receive $12,ooo per year as comPensation for your
directorship with Blue cross. You are also pai-d retirement.
benefits by Blue cross, though it is not clear from your letter
hrhether they are paid because of your directorship.
ANALYSIS
Section 871o0 nandates that I'no public official 
at any level
of state or Local government shall rnaie, participate j-n making or
in any way attenpt to use his or her official position to
influence a governnental decision in which the official-knows or
has reason to kno!, fre or she has a f inanci-al interest"'S
4 w" h..r. rnade no deterrnination whether the program will in fact
prevent profit or loss to BIue cross or any other carrier'
5 tn addition to Sectj-on 87100, you should be a!''are of Section
84308. It discusses the limitations on appointed board members'
such as your to participate in a decision if the nember has
""."pt"a, solicited or directed political contributions from a
party or particiPant in -.he case'


Thus, to answer your question, it i= .,"""=.^a-y to ask: 
(1)
Are you a public official 
under section 87100? (2) what
"o.,=Litut"! rnaking or influencingT a governmental decision? (3)
Do you have a financia] interest based on your position at Blue
Cross?
r. 
Public official
A ',pubIic official" 
is every natural person who is a member
of a stale or 1ocal governmental agrency. (Requlation 187oo(a),
copv enclosed.) rrMembertr includes unsal'aried members of boards
witfr aecis ion-rnaking authority. 
(Regrulation 18700(a) (2).) 
rrstate
aqencytr incfudes every state office, dePartnent, division, bureau,
b6ard'and cornmission. (Section a2o4g.) The Insurance Board is
part of the Business, Transportation and Housing Agency (Insurance
^code section 12710) and its decision-making authority is
established by Insurance Code Section l27lf- 
Accordingly, you are
a public offiaial 
under section 87100 because you are a member of
a toard that is part of a state agency with decision-rnaking
authority.
II. 
Governmental Decisions 
i,-:
A public official 
makes a rrgovernnentaL decisionrr if, 
when
acting iritt i., his or her official- authority, tle official. votes on
a matfer, appoints a person, obligates or commits his or her board
or cornrnission to a course of action or enters into a contract on
ils lenaff. 
(ReguLation 187oo(b).) By deciding not to do one of
these things, -the official 
would also be making a governmental
d-cision,. i.,i"=. the decision was based on a disqualifying
financiais interest and it is discLosed.6
Under certain circurnstances, a public offi-ciaI ttparticipates
in the naking of a governnental decisionrr by negotiating with a
oor"in.".rt"I entity, private Person or business entity regarding a
e;;i;i;;, 
or ry givin-g advice- or reconmendations to the decision
maker. (Regulation. 18700 (c) (2).)
A publ.ic official 
I'attempts to influence a governrnental
deci,sioir" if, for purposes of influencing the decision, he or she
coni.ct=, apPears Lefore, or otherwise attempts to- inf l-uence any
member, offi-er, 
ernployee or consultant of his or her agency'
in.g"iuti"" 
1876o.rla) copy.enclosed.) Therefore, you would be
;;ki;q a governmental decili-on at the Insurance Board j-f you were
to tri to influence any of your fellow board rnernbers '
III. 
Econonic Interest
A public otij,cial has a financial interest in a governmental'
aecisio-n ii it i= reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
;;;;-;-;.a.ria1 
financial eifect, distinguishable frorn its effect
Our File No.  I-90-656
Page 3
6  specific diSC10Sure requirements are set fOrth in RegulatiOn
■8700(b)(5).


Our File No.  ■-90-656
Page 4
on the public generall-y, on the official 
or on a ember of his or
her j-mmediate famify, or on a source of income to the official 
if
the source of j-ncome pays or promises to pay incorne totalling 
$250
or more, within l-2 months prior to the tirne when the governmental
decision is made. (section 87103(c) .)
From the facts you provided, it appears that BIue Cross is a
source of income to you of over $250 within the last 12 months.
Therefore, if it is foreseeable that a decision will material-1y
affect Bfue cross, you must disqualify yourself.
IV. Foreseeabil-itv
The effects of a decision are reasonably foreseeable if there
is a substantial likelihood that they will occur. To be
foreseeable, the effects of a decision must be more than a mere
possibility, 
though certainty is not required. 
(Do$/nev cares v.
Dov,rnev Development com. (1987) 196 cal-.App.3d 817, 983, 989-991i
witt v. Morrow (1977) 70 Cal.App.3d a17, 822; In re Thorner (1975)
1 FPPC OPs. 198. )
v. 
Materialitv
whether a decision rnaterially affects a source t6f income
turns on whether the economic interest is directlv or indirectlv
involved in the decision. A source of income is directly involved
in a decision if it initiates the proceeding in which the decision
will be nade by filing a c1aim, application, appeal or similar
request, is a named party in or subject of the proceeding, / or if
a ,inexus" exists bethleen the purpose for which the official
receives jncohe and the governmental decision. There is a nexus
between the purpose for which an official 
receives income and a
governmental- decisj.on if the officiat receives income to achieve a
goal or purpose that would be achieved, defeated, aided or
hindered by the decision. 
(Regulation 18702.1(d).)
If there is a nexus or the source of incorne is directly
invoLved, the effect of a decision j-s rnaterial. 
(Regulation
L8702.1 (a) (1).) 
Thus, you correctLy deterrnined that under the Act
you may not participate in any decision that directly involves
Blue cross because Blue cross has been a source of $250 or rnore in
incorne to you in the l-ast 12 rnonths.
Even if Bl-ue cross is not a party to or does not initiate 
the
proceedinq in which the decision wil-I be made, you must deterrnine
whether there is a nexus bet$/een the purpose for which you receive
the incone from Bfue Cross and the decision. For example, if the
purpose for vlhich Blue cross pays you to serve as a director woul-d
7 
a person or business entity is the subject of a proceeding if
a decision involves the j-ssuance, renewal, approval, denial or
revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or
contract wiah the subject business entity. 
(Regrulation
L8702.1(d) .)


our Fi■e NO.  ■-90-656
Page 5
be achieved, defeated, aided, or hindered by a deeislon before the
Insurance Board, a nexus would exists and thus the financial
effect would be material. 
(ReguLation 1a702.2(d).) PresunabLy,
Blue cross pays you for serving as a director in order to promote
its interests, manage its affairs, fu1fi11 its 1egal, obligations
as a corporation, promote its business or otherwise helP conduct
its business. You have not Provided us with enough facts
regardinq decisj-ons before the Board to determi.ne whether a nexus
exists. 
rt is possibl-e, however, that decisions concerning, for
example, the prototype contract will achieve, defeat, aid or
hinder the reasons for which Blue cross pays you to serve as a
director.
If the decision only involves Blue cross =LlllLLICgqIy, v/hether
the effect of the decision is material wilt be deternined by
Regulatj-on 18701.5, upon your characteri zation of Bl-ue cross as a
nonprofit entitY.
The effect of a decision may be material as to a nonprofit
entity that is a source of incone to a public official, 
depending
on the amount of the entity's gross annual receipts and the effect
the governmental decision witl have on the entity/s gross receipts
and its expenses. (Regulation ta7o2.5(a).) Thus, f-or example, if
BIue cross' annual- receipts are $4oo nillion or morei the effect
of an Insurance Board decision will be rnaterial if it results in
an increase or decrease in Blue cross' gross annual receipts for a
fiscal year in the amount of $1 rniLlion or more, or if .the
decision will cause BIue cross to incur or avoid additional
expenses or to reduce or eliminate existing expenses for a fiscal
yeir in the anount of $25o,ooo or nore, of if the decision wilL
iesult ir\ an 'increase or decrease in the value of BIue cross'
assets oi liabilities 
in the amount of $1 niLlion or more.
( rbid. )
I trust this letter responds to your inquiry. 
Should you
have any further questions regarding thj.s matter, please do not
hesitate to call me at (915) 322-59o'i-.
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel
By: Steven R. Rhoads
counseL, Leqal Division
